card.intuition: The set of steps the browser must perform to convert HTML, CSS, and JavaScript into pixels on the screen for the first time.
card.analogy: The 'shortest path' to opening a store: you need the keys (HTML), the sign (CSS), and the cash register (JS). You can't open until all three are ready and at the door.
when_to_use: Inlining 'critical CSS' to speed up the initial load
gotchas: Every external script or stylesheet adds another network round-trip that delays the CRP
The CRP is finished when the first frame is painted on the screen

exercise.prompt: Identify the technique of moving non-critical JS out of the Critical Rendering Path using a script attribute.
exercise.starter_code:
def optimize_path():
    # Use <script ________> to load JS without blocking HTML parsing
    attribute = "defer"
    return attribute
hints: It 'defers' the execution until the DOM is ready
exercise.test_code:
def check(ans): return ans.lower() == "defer" or ans.lower() == "async"
assert check("defer")
print("PASS")
